closed_children.title: Workspace layout: top-level Cargo.toml + crates/ skeleton
Extract balls-github-shared crate (auth, http, config, error, types)

description:
Restructure the existing balls-github-plugin repo into a cargo workspace so a
second binary (balls-plugin-github-issues, Epic B) can share auth/http/config
code without coupling roles into a single participant.

WHY: balls's plugin architecture (SPEC-lifecycle-sync-participants) is
"one participant = one name = one projection". A forge plugin and an
issue-tracker plugin are *different roles* with disjoint projections
(external.github.* vs external.github_issues.*) and want independent per-event
failure policies. The right structure is two binaries with shared library
code — not one merged participant. README §"Forge plugins vs issue-tracker
plugins" makes this explicit. This epic prepares the repo for that shape
without changing the forge plugin's observable behavior.

ACCEPTANCE: workspace builds; `make check` passes (tests + clippy + 300-line
cap + 100% coverage) across all crates; `balls-plugin-github` binary is
byte-equivalent in observable behavior (same CLI surface, same stdin/stdout
contracts, same auth-dir layout) — verified by the existing test suite
passing unchanged plus a smoke test of the install path.

Children: workspace skeleton (A1), shared crate extraction (A2),
README/Makefile rework (A3). Sequenced; each child reviewable independently.
title: Convert balls-github-plugin to a cargo workspace (two-binaries-one-workspace prep)
